en: If your company is required to maintain the relevant certification(s) outlined in this form, you will be asked to provide your certificate in a subsequent form.

The Environmental License, issued by the Ministry of Natural Resources and Environment (MiAmbiente+/SERNA) and required by General Environmental Law, Decree No. 104-93 (Article 19), is a mandatory license for all projects, activities or works that may significantly impact the environment. The law and associated regulations require an environmental impact assessment, resulting in a license, before such projects can commence. More information on the procedure of obtaining a certificate can be found here.

Does your company carry out work in Honduras in areas such as construction, industry, agriculture, or infrastructure that are considered high-risk under official regulations and require an Environmental License?
Please explain why your company is exempt from needing an Environmental License.
By providing your electronic signature, you confirm that the information submitted in this questionnaire is true, complete, and accurate in accordance with Honduran legislation.
Please select from the following reasons your company is exempt from needing an Environmental License.
The company is a sole proprietorship with no other employees.
The company does not perform work in Honduras.
